title: Lowest Common Ancestor of a Binary Search Tree
input_format: The root of a binary search tree (BST) and two nodes `p` and `q`.
output_format: The lowest common ancestor node of `p` and `q`.
constraints:
- The number of nodes in the tree is in the range `[2, 10^5]`.
- `-10^9 <= Node.val <= 10^9`
- All `Node.val` are **unique**.
- `p != q`
- `p` and `q` will exist in the BST.
function_signature: function lowestCommonAncestor(root: TreeNode | null, p: TreeNode | null, q: TreeNode | null): TreeNode | null {}
